definition.name: Artin_Wedderburn_theorem
definition.definition: (Artin-Wedderburn theorem). Every finite-dimensional semisimple algebra over an algebraically closed field (in particular, every finite-dimensional von Neumann algebra over C) is isomorphic to a direct sum of full matrix algebras: M ≅ ⊕_{j=1}^k M_{n_j}(C). Applied to the commutant π_φ(A)' when it is finite-dimensional: π_φ(A)' ≅ ⊕_{j=1}^k M_{n_j}(C), where k is the number of distinct irreducible summands in π_φ and n_j are the multiplicities. The self-adjoint part then has real dimension dim_R(π_φ(A)'^{sa}) = Σ_{j=1}^k n_j². This decomposition is the bridge that converts the geometric face dimension bound into the algebraic multiplicity bound Σ n_j² ≤ m+1. Reference: Lang, Algebra, §XVII; Takesaki, Theory of Operator Algebras I, §I.11.
